title: Data Analytics — Analyze data in businesses
summary: Traditional data warehousing (OLTP→OLAP, ETL) versus modern in-memory databases. Kinetica: GPU-accelerated database for real-time analytics. IoT and AI applications in the database.
content_html:
Data Analytics — Analyze data in businesses

Businesses need to analyze data to make business decisions. There are two main methods:

1. Traditional method: Data Warehousing

The classic model completely separates the operational database and the analytical database:

OLTP Database
Online Transaction Processing
Daily transactions, fast INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
→
ETL Process
Extract, Transform, Load
Run periodically (night/week)
→
OLAP Database
Online Analytical Processing
Data Warehouse, reporting, BI

OLTP vs OLAP

CriteriaOLTPOLAP
PurposeOperational transactionsAnalysis and reporting
Query typeINSERT/UPDATE/DELETE is shortSELECT is complex, many aggregations
DataCurrentlyHistory (years of data)
NormalizationCao (3NF)Low (star schema, denormalized)
UpdateContinuous, real-timeBatch (Recurring ETL)
For exampleOrder, payRevenue and trend reports

ETL — Extract, Transform, Load

  • Extract: Get data from OLTP and other sources
  • Transform: Clean, standardize, synthesize data
  • Load: Download to Data Warehouse

Disadvantages: Data in OLAP is always "old" (lag) compared to OLTP - usually 24 hours to 1 week. Not suitable for real-time decisions.

2. Modern method: In-Memory Databases

In-memory databases store all (or most) data in RAM instead of hard disk — hundreds of times faster to access.

Advantages of in-memory:
  • Analyze LIVE on OLTP database — no ETL required
  • Results are near real-time
  • No need for separate infrastructure for Data Warehouse
  • Suitable for IoT (continuous data streams)

3. Kinetica — GPU-Accelerated Database

Kinetica is one of the leading in-memory databases, using GPUs to process data in parallel extremely fast.

Outstanding features

  • Mature Product: Has been used in practice for many years
  • FAA (Federal Aviation Administration): Use Kinetica for Air Domain Analytics — real-time flight data analysis
  • Free Developer Edition: Can be used free of charge for personal/research purposes
  • SQL workbooks: Easy-to-use interface, demo available
  • Natural Language to SQL: Enter English question → automatically generate SQL

Natural Language Query example in Kinetica

Enter: "find all archers id and name"

Kinetica generates SQL:

SELECT ArchID, ArchName
FROM archerdata;

Results: 500 records, completed in 0.01321 seconds

Kinetica application for project

  • Load dummy data (sample data) into Kinetica
  • Run analytics: total score by archer, score by month, equipment statistics
  • Visualize results (bar chart, line chart)

4. Alternative: IOIODATA (SymphonyAI)

Another AI analytics product from SymphonyAI, however:

  • There is no free trial
  • Sector-specific: Retail, CPG, Financial Services, Industrial Manufacturing, Media, Enterprise IT
  • Need to contact to request demo

5. IoT and Streaming Databases

The Internet of Things (IoT) generates data continuously — temperature sensors, location, speed readings per second. Traditional databases are not fast enough for processing.

Popular technologies:

  • Apache Kafka: Message queue cho streaming data
  • InfluxDB: Time-series database optimized for IoT
  • TimescaleDB: PostgreSQL extension cho time-series
  • Kinetica: Real-time analysis on streaming data

6. AI trong Database

Trends in AI integration into databases:

  • Natural Language Queries: Ask in English instead of writing SQL
  • Automatic Index Suggestion: AI recommends index to create
  • Anomaly Detection: Automatically detect abnormal data
  • Predictive Analytics: Predict trends from historical data
  • Auto-tuning: Automatically optimize query execution
